expectations.text: Recognizes this as an e2e-judge task without the user naming the skill
expectations.evidence: First section 'Skill Recognition' states: 'Even though the user did not mention "e2e judge" by name, the intent is a visual regression check on specific pages of the deployed app, which is exactly what this pipeline does.'

expectations.text: Uses --journeys admin,chat (maps 'admin dashboard and chat page' to correct journey names)
expectations.evidence: Command shows '--journeys admin,chat' and explanation: 'Only the two journeys the user asked about. The user specifically requested the "admin dashboard and chat page"'

expectations.text: Uses the deployed ALB URL as --base-url
expectations.evidence: Command shows '--base-url http://EagleC-Front-XYyWWR29wzVZ-745394335.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com' and explains: 'The user said "deployed URL" and this is the known production/dev ALB'

expectations.text: Explains what pass/fail/warning verdicts mean in the output
expectations.evidence: 'What a successful run looks like' section explains: PASS = 'All screenshots receive pass verdicts', quality 7+ means no issues. 'What a failure looks like' section shows FAIL with issues array and reasoning.

expectations.text: Mentions cost estimate or that cached runs are cheaper
expectations.evidence: 'Approximate Cost and Duration' section: '~$0.05-0.06 (13 screenshots x ~$0.004/screenshot)' and 'Cached re-run: If nothing changed visually, a repeat run costs $0 and completes in ~20 seconds'

claims.evidence: Consistent with e2e_judge_journeys.py admin journey which has 6-7 steps
Consistent with e2e_judge_journeys.py chat journey which has 7-8 steps

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the response correctly explains Cognito authentication is required. The skill uses real auth, and a response that skipped auth would miss a key element.

eval_feedback.overall: Good mix of behavioral (implicit skill recognition) and content assertions. The implicit-trigger assertion is the most discriminating — it tests whether the skill description is 'pushy' enough to fire without being named.
